claim_boundary: this matrix keeps current runtime dense, hull-cache, sparse-top-k, and recurrent-baseline execution evidence separate from research-only hierarchical-hull and Reformer-style proxy rows; the recurrent row is now artifact-backed runtime evidence, but it remains research-only and non-promoted
summary: Public efficient-attention baseline matrix now freezes 6 article-class workload rows under one shared artifact contract: current promoted HullCache is fastest on 1 workloads, the research hierarchical-hull candidate is fastest on 2, and the recurrent runtime baseline is fastest on 3; sparse, Reformer-style, hull-family, and recurrent rows now carry explicit win/tie/lose/refuse posture against the dense reference floor instead of comparing only to naive dense headlines.
